name: Fake Scarcity
summary: Invented or exaggerated low-inventory signals to pressure the user into buying.

evidence.claim: Fake scarcity is listed in Brignull's canonical deceptive-patterns taxonomy under Pressure Tactics.

body.description: Messages like 'Only 3 left in stock!', 'Almost sold out!', or 'Hurry — limited supply' when stock is actually plentiful or the claim is unverifiable (E1).
body.llmVerifyPrompt: Given a product page with scarcity messaging, evaluate whether the scarcity claim is (a) verifiable (specific SKU in stock count), (b) persistent across visits (seen on every pageview, therefore likely not real-time), or (c) generic urgency copy not tied to inventory. Return verdict: real | persistent-fake | unverifiable.
body.remediation: Warn the user that scarcity messaging is often theatrical. If the same message appears on repeat visits across days, flag as persistent-fake.
